content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test crash, timeout, heartbeat loss, duplicate claim, connection loss, cancellation, stale completion, and database recovery.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Lease Fault Matrix model · Theory Feed Ts Lease…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Lease Fault Matrix precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Lease Fault Matrix model · Theory Feed Ts Lease…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 2263 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove short SKIP LOCKED claims, fenced tokens, affected-row finalize, fairness, and lease faults; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test crash, timeout, heartbeat loss, duplicate claim, connection loss, cancellation, stale completion, and database recovery.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Lease Fault Matrix model · Theory Feed Ts Lease…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: SKIP LOCKED prevents every duplicate or lost job.
